pattern: Shifting a contiguous run of letters one step toward 'a' is only ever worth doing starting at the first character that isn't already 'a', and only for as long as the run keeps improving - touching an existing 'a' or stopping early both leave value on the table.
hints: Turning an 'a' into something else only makes that position worse, and delaying an improvement at an earlier, more significant position to fix a later one instead is never better.

Find the first character that isn't 'a'. Shifting it and every following character back by one letter can only help, but the moment you reach an actual 'a' (or run out of string), stop - shifting that 'a' would turn it into 'z'.
If the string is already all 'a's, the operation still has to touch a non-empty substring, so the least damage comes from shifting only the very last character, turning it from 'a' into 'z'.

edge_inputs.description: a single non-'a' character shifts back by one letter
a single 'a' has nowhere to go but wrap to 'z'
leading 'a's are skipped before the shiftable run begins
the shiftable run is cut short by an 'a' right after it starts
an 'a' immediately follows a single shiftable character
a run of identical repeated letters all shift together
an all-'a' string only sacrifices its very last character
the extreme letter 'z' shifts down by exactly one

stress.description: near-maximum length with letters mostly random and occasional 'a' boundaries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(letters[rng.randrange(26)] if rng.random() < 0.85 else 'a' for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: near-maximum length string with no 'a' at all, forcing one single full-length run
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    letters = 'bcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(letters[rng.randrange(25)] for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
